core_behavior: The system must inspect incoming HTTP requests for the presence of required fields.
If a required field is missing, the system must return an HTTP 400 response with a structured error response body.
The error response body must identify which required fields are missing from the request.
inputs: HTTP request method (e.g., GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
A predefined set of required fields for the specific request method and endpoint
outputs: HTTP response headers: standard headers for an HTTP response (e.g., Content-Type, Date)
HTTP response body: a structured error response in a format such as JSON, containing an error code or identifier (e.g., "MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELDS")
HTTP response body: a structured error response containing a human-readable error message (e.g., "One or more required fields are missing from the request.")
HTTP response body: a structured error response containing a list or dictionary of missing required fields, with field names as keys and optional descriptions or error messages as values
assumptions: The system has a predefined set of required fields for each endpoint and request method, which can be retrieved from a configuration file, database, or other data store.
The system uses JSON as the format for the structured error response body.
The system assumes that the request body is in a format that can be parsed (e.g., JSON, form data).
The system will not attempt to validate the request body beyond checking for the presence of required fields.
failure_modes: If the system is unable to parse the request body, it will return an HTTP 400 response with a generic error message (e.g., "Invalid request body").
If the system encounters an internal error while processing the request, it will return an HTTP 500 response with a generic error message (e.g., "Internal Server Error").
If the system is unable to retrieve the predefined set of required fields, it will return an HTTP 500 response with a generic error message (e.g., "Internal Server Error").
edge_cases: An empty request body: the system will return an HTTP 400 response with a list of all required fields as missing.
A request body with unknown or unexpected fields: the system will ignore these fields and only report missing required fields.
A request with a missing or invalid Content-Type header: the system will attempt to parse the request body based on the assumed format (e.g., JSON).
A request with a large or malformed request body: the system will return an HTTP 400 response with a generic error message (e.g., "Invalid request body").
security_constraints: The system must not reveal sensitive information about the required fields or the internal implementation of the system in the error response.
The system must not allow an attacker to deduce the presence or absence of specific required fields through timing or error message analysis.
performance_constraints: The system must respond to requests with missing required fields within a reasonable time limit (e.g., 100ms).
The system must be able to handle a high volume of requests with missing required fields without significant performance degradation.
The system must not consume excessive resources (e.g., memory, CPU) when processing requests with missing required fields.
